method_atom: A disorder-driven continuous (second-order) transition of an eigenstate ensemble of a random linear operator between a spatially-extended, transport-permitting regime and a spatially-confined, localized regime; at the critical point the eigenstates are scale-invariant and exhibit multifractal (anomalous, position-dependent multi-scaling) statistics. Universality is organized by a symmetry classification of the random operator, with critical behaviour captured through renormalization-group / field-theoretic critical theories, random-matrix ensembles (e.g. power-law random banded matrices), coarse-grained network/lattice models, and numerical finite-size scaling.
object_atom: Single-particle quantum states of electrons in disordered solids (disordered electronic systems), specifically the metal-insulator and quantum-Hall-type transitions between metallic and localized phases, described by random Dirac and other disordered Hamiltonians.
brief_reason: The disorder-induced localization/delocalization critical transition with multifractal critical states and symmetry-class universality is the transferable machinery, while the electrons-in-disordered-solids system and its metal-insulator / quantum-Hall transitions are the physics-specific object.
